reviews.review_text: Bought the game to play with my brothers. Couldn't stop playing. Almost missed work the following day.

This was fun during the early progression, but as you go farther, it doesn't feel like there are meaningful rewards. It all feels a little bland and repetitive. Either way, It was worth the money for the 15 or so hours of enjoyment.

I can see many reasons why this game isn't for everyone. It fells addicting playing for the first time; the game has great atmosphere, musics and visuals. The new biomes that you discover and items that you find gets you hooked into the game. However, after playing for a while the game tends to be a bit repetitive and tedious which causes a burn-out from the game, if you are not a big of fan of these types of games. You generally tend to find yourself unable to mine the new biome stones and ores basically, which forces you to grind for the better tools. There is always new biomes so you do this for most of the game. As a result of this the new biomes that you find tend to be less interesting as the game progresses. Progression-vise I find it to be a bit unoptimized as you mostly get jumpscared and one shotted by bosses while exploring (Don't get me wrong its fun :=), also the rewards you get from bosses arent that great generally, The random stuff you get from mobs are far better for some reason. The bosses are also very straightforward generally and they dont last very long.  If you really like grinding for good items, and mining new ores always this is the perfect game. Anyhow, I believe there's a lot that this game lacks in the long term, but I'll guarantee most of you that you're going to find it very enjoyable  for your first couple of hours that's why I strongly recommend giving it a shot.

I don't really enjoy this game and I think i'm kind of done with it.

I think it has a lot of neat ideas and cute sprites and things, but I feel like I was misled into believing it was something that it was not.

The advertising for this game made me think you'd be building up a base, gathering resources to that end, getting automation for things, farming, animal husbandry, etc. Lots of videos on the steam page love to show you zipping around on boats and things. I was imagining setting up a self sustaining base, farming for food you need to survive, defending your base from monsters, etc.

Does this game have those things? "Technically" it does. It's not explicitly a lie, but it's greatly misleading. Boats and ALL forms of automation are gated behind special resources you don't get until late game. You have to defeat 3 bosses before you even get access to the areas that yield those resources.

The base building itself seems redundant and unnecessary. Why farmin when you are already flooded with abundant food just from killing things while exploring, which you are required to do anyway? It's not like an optional alternative, you MUST explore, you MUST kill, so you automatically collect food this way, you simply don't need to farm. So farming for food is pointless and redundant. Now food does heal you, and is in fact more or less the only way to heal in the game because you have no base health regeneration and items having it are both very rare and very weak. Only other thing you have are potions and sleeping. I feel like carrying a bed around with you and sleeping when you take damage is perhaps the best way to heal, but that seems so cheaty and ridiculous. The rest of farming, and animal husbandry for that matter, just gives you resources for things you don't really need. Most of it involves building more advanced crafting tables which just in turn you use for more advanced crafting tables, and you don't really end up using any of them. At least I didn't. Why craft gear when enemies drop better stuff pretty regularly?

I feel like you dont need a base at all, you can just plow your way through the game with your pickaxe and the only structure you ACTUALLY need is the scrap/repair table because its the only way to repair your gear and, oh yeah, by the way, all your gear takes damage constantly so you have to repair everything basically every trip you make out. That's not very fun, especially because it's so cheap to repair the stuff it feels more like a chore than some kind of challenge or something. So you just cruise through the debts with a bed and repair table, using stuff you find to kill everything, and there's really no point making a base.

I also thought the base would be attacked sometimes and need to be defended, but it doesn't happen. Enemies don't attack, they don't really do anything except kind of drunkenly wander around aimlessly unless you aggro them. So you can build spike traps, and eventually turrets (which, of course, are gated behind end game stuff), but it's pointless because you don't need it. In fact, even if enemies did attack, you can make your base invincible just by digging a pit around it. Walls can be destroyed but enemies can't figure out crossing water. You can make a bridge leading in and out of your base, and destroy it when you leave and rebuild t when you come back, instantly, to be totally secure. And if you have a boat, you could just ride it across. Enemies can't do anything about that.

I guess the game is fun enough for people who like this sort of "adventure through randomly generated content" kind of dungeons, but i'm more of a base builder and automation person, so  was left sorely disappointed by everything relating to that being end game only, extremely expensive, and also just kind of pointless in the first place. Buildings need to be powered to operate, but you can produce infinite power from a handful of different things you can build. There is no cost to operate, no logistics, no special components, nothing, just free limitless power. 

I really wanted to like this game, but it became more and more obvious as time went on that nothing was going to change. I wanted a creative sandbox, instead I got generic pixel art adventure game that feels like base building was tacked on and gated.

I don't think the game is a "Bad" game per se, it's just a bad game for me specifically, and I do feel rather misled by the way it's advertised on the steam page into thinking it was something it isn't. You basically just mine lines through rock, diverting to mine ore, and killing monsters you come across, until you get to one of the bosses, kill it, then repeat the process for the next boss.

I thought the music puzzles were neat though.

Played this with my husband. Completely lost track of time and ended up not eating all day. We literally looked up at the clock after a restroom break and said, "oh crap it's already 2pm!" Great game 10/10 would recommend.

this game lends a very simple calmness that i have only encountered in games like Minecraft and taskbar farmers, this is by far a good thing. take your time and make a core world and adventure world, maybe even a farming world this game lends itself to monopolizing on your time but not your money. i would happily buy DLC but the developers care and because of that i do too.
woah, incredible game, very chill and packed with exploration, hints of terraria meets stardew valley, also banging soundrack

Jack of all trades, master of none. I really wanted to like this game, but I just couldn't. The lackluster progression system makes the whole experience feel like a pointless grind. Furthermore, the biomes are completely identical outside of simple color swaps, which makes exploration feel incredibly boring.
A very novel open-world experience. Explore as the circular map expands. It has the potential to develop into a game like Terraria. And the gameplay feels like a 2D version of Mashin Hero Wataru.
Great game, but hasn't worked on my Linux machine since December 2025. Nothing after version 1.1.2.0 works for me.

These types of games are addictive but I come out of it feeling bored and jaded by the end of it, forager was similar. The majority of time I've spent is just grinding and walking back and forth then after a while I look at the wiki to see where to go, I tried to do without it but I'm just walking in circles, it tells me to find some ♥♥♥♥ in some unknown place and do some ♥♥♥♥ I didn't even know possible and I'm just out of it.
The beggining to these 'survival' games feels so freeing and vast, you're wondering where to go, making decisions, but it wears off deeply after 2 hours. I might just be jaded with the genre but if you're like me and were once fascinated by that original thrill, just know it's fleeting. I cannot reccomend this game.

A seriously underrated masterpiece. this game has something special that keeps pulling me back. i can easily melt hours and hours into this game and have an absolute blast. its the perfect game to come home to after a long day and get lost in. the music is so chill and i found myself vibing to the it when im not even playing the game. the devs have been constantly updating the game every single month consistently and and actually listen to the community when it comes to quality of life and content, amazing stuff. new bosses, weapons, armor, biomes every time i come back to this game. a breath of fresh air in a otherwise corporate greed gaming industry. not a dollar wasted.

the game feels super sluggish in terms of progress, fights, building, etc.
everything feels weak, every enemy deals massive damage (don't even think about "hard mode", there is virtually no upside, just frustration), player movement is really weak, minions are stupid, mana weapons are just worse ranged weapons, I could go on and on
this game is not worth the price in the slightest

Awesome game, awesome creators, awesome updates. This game never ceases to amaze me. It has enough difficult challenges, AND enough fun for everyone. You can complete solo, or with friends. Highly recommended.
Love this game, similar feel to terraria, stardew, and overall I would classify this as another classic that I will return to time and time again. 10/10

I have some misgivings about this game, but overall, I would recommend, especially if it goes on sale.

This game starts out strong. It's like a terraria-esque top down game. It's really fun to explore the caves, see different funky enemies and bosses, and try out new armors and builds. It gets even more fun with friends (some of the bosses are quite hard solo).

The game falls off hard around mid to late game. The variety in late game weapons drops dramatically (not armor sets though weirdly enough). It's like the developer got tired of coming up with new ideas for weapons and gave up on it. The game practically forces you into a melee build, because every other damage type is so inferior to the late game "legendary" sword, even other legendary weapon types. Plus, the majority of armor sets buff melee damage and attack speed.

Also, farming hostile mobs for drops is quite a hassle because of the way spawning mechanics work. 

And for those of you who are achievement hunters, be prepared to spend many many hours grinding out levels for every single skill tree in the game. Also, you'll have to create at least two, likely more worlds in order to collect all the needed crafting materials for rare items that for some wild reason actually have a decent chance of never spawning.

These are mostly late game issues however, and I still easily got over three hours of entertainment per dollar spent, so in my book that's pretty well worth it. Just know that the early game is a lot of wonder and fun, and the mid/late game becomes a grind.
Super fun game. A sandbox a would suggest to anyone who want to feel a good exploring game.

I love this game, I could easily spend hours of my day playing this over several games in the genre. However, the biggest reason why I haven't played it much since its release is the lack of crossplay/the developers saying they have no plans to add crossplay. This game is meant to be played with other people, the adventure is meant to be shared with a friend or family member. I originally bought this game for PC while it was still in very early access I've recommended it to several people, I showed them videos of gameplay and they were very excited to get it, but after seeing that it has no crossplay plans, those people decided not to get it. Everyone has different consoles that they prefer to play on, not everyone is financially able to get every console. 

The developers have said that it would be incredibly difficult to make crossplay happen, but I think they are shooting themselves in the foot by not investing in that aspect of the game. They could have sold a lot more copies of the game if people knew they would be able to play together across the 4 different consoles it was released on.
Very chill and user-friendly, entertaining and not crazily grind-y. Multiplayer also works well, I never lagged playing this or had any kinds of problems. Very smooth gameplay, good game.

really fun and cozy. 

SOME boss battles are kinda hell.

cheesing is/was easy on melee/range with scarab armor set buffs and the chakram, but imo it's more fun with multiplayer. 

Though it's a really good game singleplayer too, I just personally get way more scared and/or bored sooner when I play games alone

Implement this farming mechanic and equipment graphics flamethrower lightning to romestead. I bought it with 40% as a bundle with 10% discount. with steam punk quality of life, auto drill steam engine etc. I play this instead core keeper while waiting for your devs to finish the romestead. and zombie attack their is boring compare to this. as the boss lurking here tunnel, water, showing fear factor.
this game is super epic and i look forward to future updates. its got survival mechanics and all that cool stuff. its kinda like stardew but cool. idk i find i have more fun with this than the other.

I love this game. Got it on switch as well due to not being to familiar on pc. It is really cute. Stylish, a bit like terraria, fun bosses to fight, mysteries, a bit like a town builder. You can transfer stuff between world with characters. I kinda have terraformed the middle circle some times, it gets really pretty. I don't know if they fixed that enemy spawning if you terrafom the worm nest area to grass, cause that was annoying. Fun solo as well as with others.
Pretty solid, if you like Terraria and want a more unforgiving combat experience this game is fire.
This is too fun and addictive, please, send help.
I had NOT expected the game to be this good! Feel is similar to Terraria or Starbound but with isometric view. Not too hard. Easy to explore. Ambience is a real treat. The lighting is [i]perfect.[/i]
Played on the Playstation but wanted to play around with the world seed, etc. so purchased on Steam. Hey, I've paid for it on two systems so the game must be doing something right.

A Terraria-like Sandbox Boss Rush game with RPG elements/build classes and very pretty visuals and music. Has ample room to grow and improve especially with the mod support. This is game is a 'Keeper' down the road ahead.
A lot of love and creativity went into this game. Especially with the sunken sea.
amazing game. super fun and can spend hours in here.
Calm and nice progression with a nice difficulty aspect with it
pretty good game. just ets a little boring after playing every day. might just be a me problem. DO get it and DO buy all your friends it s that you can all play togetherr and kill people as warlocks

Super underrated game
There are so much things to do and it's got a lot of convenient stuff in it, the aesthetic and lighting of the game is nice to look at. I am seriously digging this game, I recommend playing it with a friend or you can play it solo whichever one's enjoyable. The game is also comfortable, the bosses are hard and cool. This game's just subterranean terraria.
This felt like playing terraria for the first time. I really enjoyed this game so far. I do wish mapping was shared between friends.

Play game with my wife, we fight creatures and mine around the world, I die a lot but better me than her. ADHD trap of a game but in the best way possible. Dungeon crawler game with a feel of like terraria and stardew but basically if stardew was just beating the crap out of things in a DnD dungeon.
very fun and addicting game with tons of content to do!

Do yourself the favor and experiment with the world building scales. I love the fact that I can customize and remake worlds over and over till I get the one I like best without having to remake the character as well!

Core Keeper is fantastic. The automation present in the game relieves a large amount of grinding late game allowing the player to continue exploring and enjoying while the drills do the grinding for you!

So much depth and discovery. Do yourself a favor though and don't look up guides when you play and you'll be hopelessly addicted. Animal Crossing, Phantasy Star Online, and Link to the Past rolled into one. 10/10

so goated bro, love this game. like terraria if it was top down but so much more at the same time (still love terraria btw)

literally played for 10hrs one time instead of studying so goated absolutely worth it
Slept on game with so much playability well worth the money super fun with friends too

This game is an absolute banger! I turned it on thinking I'd just mine for 30 minutes, and suddenly it's 4 AM, birds are chirping outside, and I'm still digging. The whole exploration system is just super satisfying. That feeling when you break through a wall into a whole brand new biome is totally addictive. The bosses are tons of fun and a great challenge, and building your base feels so good. Plus, there are tons of ways to customize and decorate it exactly how you want. There is always so much to do and you never get bored. You can go fishing, cook meals for buffs thats must do or you will have bad time, move NPCs into your base that will sell you items  or even get your own cute pets that will fly around your base or go with you give you buffs.
 
Small warning it's a pretty massive grind. With every single new biome you discover, the difficulty spikes like crazy. By the time you reach the endgame, the scaling is insane it becomes a real struggle just to stay alive and kill anything. If you want to hunt down all the achievements or get those top-tier special weapons, you're going to sweat. I spent literally several hours just trying to craft or drop certain items.
Fun game, love to play with my son, I wish graphics where a little better even for the style chosen, but can't complain, I'll still enjoy

A bit of a slow burn, but once you get going killing bosses improving your gear and leveling up skill points is some of the most addicting gameplay that I've found, I love this game to bits it's probably my favorite survival game
Honestly, this is as close as you can get to a 3d Terraria. Its a game with a slew of content, and is still getting substantial updates. Its worth the price, especially with friends! 10/10
good game if you like exploration and getting stronger

This game is very good and not sure why it's so slept on. Games that it reminds me of at times while never feeling like a knock-off of either. 
-Stardew Valley (outside of the dating/life sim which is totally absent in CK), 

There are some there are some excellent QoL features related to inventory management and farming too. 

Very fun automation and combat. I play solo. The music is excellent. Decent amount of replayability due to classes and skill trees though I'm still on first toon.
Rewarding exploration, great build options for whatever your play style is, challanging boss fights without making it seem impossible. Amazing game that will continue to entertain me.
Cool game with nice graphics and cute animals!

Phenomenal game.
Beautiful graphics, unique bosses and endless collectables. I've played this game 3 different times and am still finding new things as I unlock the map. Updates keep the game play fresh and exciting. Definitely a hidden gem game.

This game was something I needed in a PC game. For a while, I never felt like a "true gamer" if I kept playing the big games all the time. When I heard about this gem, I knew that this is where home is. Core Keeper, you kept me where I lost my core until I found it. Thank you.
Adventure, farming, dungeons, collecting. There is a lot you can do in this game. I like the diversity and would recommend it.
spent more time retrieving my items than actually playin the game and some of the hours that i spent on this game were the most grueling rageporn i've ever experienced

This game is easily in my top 5 and to those complaining about how difficult it is from the get go, this isn't the game for you. Core Keeper thinks of everything you could possibly imagine, with one of the best quality of life titles I have played so far. What really makes me happy is the developers and how they go out their way to update and fix any problems that arise. The content they push out is nothing short of brilliant. Sure, lag is a little bit of an issue for my friend who plays with me but it doesn't destroy our experience one bit as we only get lag if we are too far apart. Core Keeper has ALWAYS been a game that I come back too and I feel this is an ode to the developers.

I love this game so much that I would be willing to pay for DLC but I understand that some of the community might not agree with me which is fine. Thank you so much to the devs for making such an amazing wee game that me and my friends love to play together :D 

Peace out \/
A fun underground Minecraft esc game with actual plot but enough free roaming to forget it if you want to. is Stardew had no npcs and added simple building and more exploring to it.
Very fun and relaxing. Great artstyle, enjoyable gameplay and story is neat.

**EQUIP**
Lots of armor, equipment, and weapon choices with different playstyles.

**BOSS**
The bosses are unique and each fight feels fresh and different while exploring.

**MAP**
Many biomes to explore, each with their own theme and bosses.

**CRAFTING**
Crafting is simple, easy to understand, and not too complicated.

**FOOD BUFF**
Food buffs are unique. You can’t just stack random buffs because each food gives different effects.

**FISHING & FARMING**
Fishing and farming are important parts of the game. You can’t really get the best food buffs without fishing, so it feels almost mandatory if you want the strongest buffs and food combinations.

**SKILL SYSTEM**
Skill points are pretty varied and let you build your character in different ways.

**PET**
Pets can even have different stats from the same egg, which makes them more interesting.

**BUILDING**
Building is fun with lots of tile colors and decoration choices, even in a top-down game style.

**ENDGAME**
Sadly, the endgame weapon meta feels too predictable. The Core Keeper Stormbringer build is just way stronger than almost everything else.
I picked this up randomly because it looked like a top down Terraria+Stardew Valley and now I have so many friends hooked on this game. Absolutely recommend with friends.

This game has been such an amazing experience since the start. This game scratches a certain itch for me that similar games just dont. The gameplay and progression are great. the devs show they came by continually showing update after update that they go above and beyond.

There are many things for you to do. The combat is fun and engaging. The survival aspect is great. You can play in so many ways. Also, the map is incredibly large, and there are many biomes to explore. It's so incredibly simple and fun. I got addicted to it immediately.

From what little of the game I played it seemed fine. Has a cool scrapping mechanic. I ran into a wall of progression quite quickly because the bosses were very hard to defeat in multiplayer. I think there is some underlying issues with the game that need work like some creatures and items but most people probably wouldn't care about it if they didn't look too closely. 

I am not particularly a fan of how reliant bosses are to progress because I couldn't defeat a boss but I felt like I couldn't do anything else. But yeah, different strokes for different folks.

The core gameplay is satisfying, i do wish there was more loot variety as endgame gear is essentially dependent on about a dozen items and if you dont have any of those endgame items, it just becomes harder to beat (still doable though) there are alot of satisfying mechanics this game provides and it was an overall positive and fun experience
A game for all moms and dads. Very accessible very painful, very fun.

I can imagine why people love this game.
And this review will be contradictory. Cause in the end I lost the fun to play it.
So to recommend playing Solo, maybe. In Multiplayer, probably better.

The graphics are really nice and cute ideas, a lot to build a nice base and stuff.
For someone just wanting to chill and build nice areas, this is probably a nice game.
For someone that wants to explore, mine, craft, fight and progress, it loses its spirit, cause there is little variety, of the stuff you do every area. Just the graphics and monsters change.
Also, it's (probably) so much better playing with other players, rather than solo, like I did. It gets tedious and grindy playing solo.

Overall negative points for me were:
- Too few weapons/armors. Or rather too much farming needed to get anything useful, or unique, except from the 3 weapons and single armor you can craft, after you reached the next area... Farming bosses is tough and tedious, when playing solo. Farming monsters for their stuff, takes ages, except you build a super optimized monster farm, which takes also too much time, for the results, when the armors/weapons getting replaced in the next area anyways.
Like how do you get an armor to play a minion build, you can't craft any of them, the crafting ones are just boosting melee or ranged damage. For minion, mage, explosion damage boosts you need to farm specific monsters or bosses and hope for a lucky DROP, you can not craft them with the monster drop material, which all of them have, but you can not craft their armors or weapons, you just have to hope for a drop, till you hopefully any time soon have all three armor pieces, or accessories collected, to fulfill the setbonus requirement...
So I play almost the whole game with the same weapon type, cause I had no luck with boss drops, or chest contents... When compared to Terraria, which many do, it has so limited fighting variety.
- When talking about fighting, we come to the controls... They are not entirely bad, but rather spongy, also the keybindings, like to open chest is really annoying after some time, when you again destroyed half your chests and you have to pick up hundreds of items... And also missing keybindings, for quickly use healing items, or buffs, is also a quality feature that is strongly needed...Which would make fighting bosses solo so much less chaotic, cause the buffs run out faster than you can kill these bosses soloing, and you have to constantly change the item you're holding to use them, or the bars to get your buffs back up. 
All this stuff you will not be effected of that much at the beginning, but the longer you play, it just gets tedious and you start questioning why keep playing, the fun is gone.
- Also it's nice to have a ton of items to decorate with, but when exploring getting your inventory cluttered with all these bushes and stuff is contra to the point of these type of games...
- For a random generated open world, this game is really quite linear tho... Always getting the map marker where you have to go next, you just run from one side of the map to the other one... Exploring is just for the hope to find any good loot to gear up (which I was maybe just unlucky with) Or farm ore until you get enough stuff to farm the boulders. And you need a lot of bars to move that ore to your base, when you don't want to run around constantly to collect them all over the place... The progressing is so steamlined.

I can imagine many of these points being less annoying when playing together with other players. But solo it adds up.

Also modding exists and all build in the game, but it's sadly not really big, really few mods, and a lot of them broken...

This game is like Terraria turned boring. None of the bosses are interesting or unique and all of them feel like filler to pad the game out. At its core, core keeper is a game about exploring and pretty much solely that, just incredibly boring stuff. Might be fun if you're not great at videogames

The first 1-2 hours are fun, past that it's ♥♥♥♥. First 2 bosses are thought out also. Game needs an overhaul, not worth $20.
not a dungeon keeper like, also not really all that fun or interesting
if you like melee or ranged combat its a good game if you like summoning or magic it is a complete train reck

Totally disappointed with this game. The beginning is incredible, the first boss fights are great, and there are lots of new mechanics. It really reminded me of the good old days playing Terraria.

But the further you progress, the more you realize the game is completely unbalanced. The clearly broken class is magic. I played ranger, and I ended up using the same musket and armor for about 3/4 of my playthrough. Really disappointing.

What made it even worse is that the final boss fight is just unnecessarily long. One of the most boring boss fights I’ve ever done in any game.

The only positive points are the early game and the graphics. In short: save your money and just play Terraria instead.

One of the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ coop game that I've ever tried to play. Online is fully laggy - all time I was in some kind of guessing who is gonna crush first me or my girlfriend. What is more funnier is looking how it's getting worst just by building more stuff in house. Wanna new room in house? Then get freaking slideshow after making it. 

Even without online things this game is just bad. They can not just fix ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ bug-swapping of ur stuff in the inventory bar for more than 5 year (check steam's discussions). Only this 1 part make this game unplayable for me. 

There is no balance in this game at all. U can play with any armor - all of it just sucks. wtf I'm dying from oneshots with 800+hp in tank set on normal difficulty? Most of weapons sucks too. Some random magic staff is better than freaking legendary sword and bow obtained from long quests lines (which is just looting of some ♥♥♥♥ for 10+ hours).

If u wanna something similar but without all of this minuses buy necesse, it's cheaper and just better at all.

My biggest problem with this game is how disappointing "farming" is. Enemies spawn in waves, in which the game checks for valid spawn tiles and then *might* spawn an enemy on it. The waves are about 20 minutes apart. There is a way to automate ore mining. You need some pricey mining equipment to maximise ore output but it's not linear. One mining tool gives 0.23 ore per minute. The second still doubles it to 0.46 ore per minute. With 8 tools it's 1.33 ore per minute, while it *should* be 1.84

The game constantly punishes trying to min/max or lets you quickly find out that it just isn't worth it to build a farm. Yes, technically having a farm is better than having no farm, but it's not really satisfying.
THE. GRIND. IS. REAL. Late game gets very boring and grindy with both class items and normal craft items. Everything also does a ton of damage which can make things feel unbalanced.
Bought This game at 1 am and played till 4.30am straight peak. must have if you find friends or just buy some and then play with your bought friends or something idk.
I'm not a big gamer, but this is very approachable, but has enough going on and enough depth to keep me comming back. I'm really enjoying playing with my boyfriend in co-op mode.

I will never get to play this game because mods are broken or something. Game is just rebooting over and over and over again. What is the point of an in game mod manager if it does not manage mods?

Have now tried several times in 2025 and 2026. If a mod is broken please remove it! Is there no one moderating the mods?

Its an ok game just really dang difficult for no reason, pretty much everything will do ridiculous damage to you oh you have 300 hp and rarity 7-8 gear the bug enemies will still 3 shot you if you get hit and thats still in the beginning areas of the game. Overall game feels unbalanced.

This is by far one of my favorites. I always come back, and loose hours while playing. 

The devs are actively listening to feedback and have made some amazing changes for QOL in gameplay. I mean come on - All I need to do is hit Q in my storage room and my inventory clears itself out to its assigned spots. There's still things left unbalanced, such as the magic, but I know those will all get fixed with time. I love exploring new worlds, finding new scenes, building up my bases. I can't wait to see what else they do with this.

Even better with a friend to hop on with! Absolutely a 10/10.
cool game but walking 3 minutes and getting oneshot and then having to walk 3 minutes back unless you remembered to pick up your minecart and keep it in your hotbar is annoying
my compuker is a potato and this runs great very fun highly recommend
good overall gameplay, not bad

I'll be frank up front: this rating is unfair. However, it's also fair in it's unfairness. 

 Did you know that because of quirks of procedural generation and its clashes with this game's insistence upon static setpieces and boundaries, it's actually possible for the "segment" of the world that spawns a boss to just not appear in your world, ever? And that locks you out of engaging that boss, in that world, entirely? 

We spent hours combing a featureless desert full of infinitely spawning trash mobs, looking for a boss the game insisted existed, a boss that was gating one of the final progress steps left in our world, and found out only afterward that it's possible for it to just not appear. The suggestion to simply move inventory to a new world and rush the boss is frankly insulting. Utter garbage.

So much of the design of this game seems like compromise born out of the underbaked idea of semi-static procedural generation. The boss scanners, as well as the summoning totems, the weirdly persistent character inventory, and the way enemies just endlessly appear out of thin air from certain ground coverings. Pick a lane and stick to it, this has all of the disadvantages of procgen with none of the benefits. Give me my time back.

to preface im only setting my recommendation to "not" to off set the "very positive" majority. 

Basically just play terraria. I just find this game feature rich but not feature complete. there are so many aspects of the game like various biomes or classes or crafting but I feel like I either have to sink sooo much time finding everything (in a repetitive dull manor) or it feels like they made this feature because it sounded cool but couldn't fully implement it to stand up to everything else. 

**Overall** Id say you should try the game but dont have expectations of a similar game to terraria

♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ MOD.IO,
the game use weird modding sites, it's neither good, or responsive.
Both if you are mod user or mod dev, you will have hard time,

Long post:
In game menu you could click Mods button, this will go to Mod io page, but this is very clunky to use with mouse and keyboard, the scroll feel like console, the menu and button is way way too big, AND you need to connect your steam with mod io, this would be fine if it's working, but no, I just get 30 mins of not responding, and then "Something went wrong" error. 
when you close the game and open it again, it ask AGAIN to login to your steam!!! bruh.

I tried several times to get into this game.  It just... is a bit too hard to get into.  I've got 4 hours in game and never found anything interesting.  Never managed to figure out how to progress technology past copper and a bit of iron.

I'm sure if you are willing to look up guides and watch playthroughs, the game probably has ways to figure this out but... I just don't have the patience for it, and spending this long in the game with it providing nothing just soured me on the whole experience.  This is also really a multiplayer experience for the most part.  As a single player game it is a bit empty and soulless from what I can tell.

I think games like Necesse give a much better and more approachable introduction that get you into the game.  If you just want to do crafting, Terraria is also more easily accessible.

Not a fan of this one, but your mileage may vary.
Imagine Terraria, but it looks like Stardew Valley, and it takes place on Hoxxes
This game I don't think it's for me. There's base building to it, but I don't fully know why. Granted I didn't play very long but it just seems off.
Surprisingly good survival craft that blends many of the best features from Terraria and Valheim. I love the music and atmosphere, would highly recommend to any survival craft fans

One of the better hidden gems out there, if you like Terraria you will absolutely love this.
The game was boring, it just wasn't for me. I roam around and killed some things but it just wasn't engaging.
Really well made, many fun, very game. AWESOME references to other games too.

I've had CoreKeeper since early access, and I've loved playing it even as of now. I'm really glad multiplayer doesn't cap at 4 players cause I usually have 5-6 friends who like these sort of games and play with me on them. 
I look forward to the new updates, and I got to day I love the music
good game, really good graphics and soundtrack. the bosses felt really easy past the three main titans though, theres a massive amount of content.

This game is fun for alot of reasons. If you are down to just chill and grind a game in peace its very relaxing, and also challenging at times. If you are wanting to play with friends the multiplayer is seamless and easy to connect friends into your world or visit theirs. They recently had an update from the time that im playing now and the last time i played they did a really good job at adding quality of life elements and making the game even more fun! I dont usually leave reviews and this game has compelled me to. if you are a fan of Terraria or Minecraft this will be a game that you will want to try there are some Nods to each game that you will enjoy.
Great and really fun game!
This game is Amazing!! Found this game through youtube and man!!! IT IS ADDICTIVE!!
Lots of fun, worth $20 especially with friends!
